servinfo.title: Main Bearing, Crankshaft and Cylinder Block Removal and Installation

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Main Bearing, Crankshaft and Cylinder Block Components

s1.ptxt: Remove engine assembly from vehicle.
Remove flywheel (M/T model) or drive plate (CVT model).
Remove pistons and connecting rods.
Remove engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing (1).
Remove main bearing cap No.1 and No.2 bolts in numerical order gradually as indicated in figure.
Remove main bearing caps.
Remove crankshaft from cylinder block.
Remove sensor plate and spring pin from crankshaft, if necessary.
Remove piston cooling valves and nozzles, if necessary.
Remove venturi plug, if necessary.

item.ptxt: The engine will seize up if it is operated when the parts listed below are not properly lubricated.
Be sure to lubricate all the friction surfaces of these parts with engine oil before installing them.
Using main bearing cap bolts that are deformed exceeding the service limit could lead to engine problems.
When main bearing cap bolts are reused, make sure that main bearing cap bolts are not deformed exceeding the service limit referring to “Main Bearing Cap Bolt” under Main Bearing Inspection .

s1.ptxt: Install venturi plug (1) and tighten venturi plug to specified torque, if removed.
Install sensor plate (1) using the following procedure, if removed.

s2.ptxt: Install sensor plate to crankshaft, spring pin into hole (4) and facing inside edge (5) outward as shown in figure.

s1.ptxt: Install piston cooling nozzles (1) and valves (2). Tighten piston cooling valves to specified torque, if removed.

attention3.ptxt: Install piston cooling valve while matching position cooling nozzle to positioning (3) of cylinder block when installed piston cooling valve.

s1.ptxt: Install main bearings to cylinder block and main bearing cap using the following procedure.

item.ptxt: Install main bearing halves (1) with oil groove (2) to cylinder block.
Install other main bearing halves to main bearing cap.

s2.ptxt: Fit the tab (1) on each bearing into groove (2) in cylinder block and main bearing cap.
Press bearing end (3) until it fully seat in cylinder block and main bearing cap.
Apply engine oil to sliding surface (4) of main bearing halves.

attention4.ptxt: If oil is present between the bearing’s outside surface (5) and the cylinder block or main bearing cap surface, the bearing could heat up to very high temperature, resulting in seizure.
Be sure to thoroughly wipe off any trace of oil that is present between the bearing’s outside surface (5) and the cylinder block or main bearing cap surface.

s1.ptxt: Install thrust bearings (1) to cylinder block between No.2 and No.3 cylinders.

attention3.ptxt: Be sure to face oil groove (2) side to crank web.

s1.ptxt: Confirm that dowel pins (3) are installed to intake side of each journal.
Install crankshaft to cylinder block.
Install main bearing cap as follows.

attention4.ptxt: Using main bearing cap bolts that are deformed exceeding the service limit could lead to engine problems.
Before installing main bearing cap, make sure that main bearing cap No.1 bolts are not deformed exceeding the service limit referring to “Main Bearing Cap No.1 Bolt” under Main Bearing Inspection .

s2.ptxt: Point arrow mark on cap to crankshaft pulley side. Fit them sequentially in ascending order, 1, 2, 3, 4 and 5, starting from pulley side.
Apply engine oil to bolt thread and seat of main bearing cap No.1 bolt.
Tighten main bearing cap No.1 bolts (“1” – “10”) to 30 N·m (3.1 kgf-m, 22.5 lbf-ft) according to numerical order as shown using a 12-point socket wrenches.
In the same manner as in Step c), tighten them to 50 N·m (5.1 kgf-m, 37.0 lbf-ft).
Turn all main bearing cap No.1 bolts 60° according to numerical order in figure.
Tighten main bearing cap No.2 bolts (“11” – “18”) to 25 N·m (2.5 kgf-m, 18.5 lbf-ft) according to numerical order as shown.

s1.ptxt: Check that crankshaft rotates smoothly when turning it by 12 N·m (1.2 kgf-m, 9.0 lbf-ft) torque or below.
Clean sealing surface between oil seal housing (2) and cylinder block.
Apply engine oil to new rear oil seal (1) and install it to engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing (2) using special tool.

spec.title: Engine rear crankshaft oil seal installing position (depth)

s1.ptxt: Install dowel pin (2) to rear oil seal housing (1) as shown in figure.
Apply sealant to mating surface of rear oil seal housing (1).

spec.title: Sealant amount for rear oil seal housing

s1.ptxt: Apply engine oil to oil seal lip, then install engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing (1) using special tool.
Tighten new engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing bolts (2) to specified torque.

attention3.ptxt: Confirm that engine rear crankshaft oil seal lip is not turned over after engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing is installed.

torqueitem.t-part: Engine rear crankshaft oil seal housing bolt

s1.ptxt: Install flywheel (M/T model) or drive plate (CVT model).
Install pistons and connecting rods.
Install cylinder head.
Install camshafts and tappets.
Install timing chain.
Install timing chain cover.
Install cylinder head cover.
Install oil pan.
Install engine assembly to vehicle.
